tool_input.summary: No dedicated Saturn plasmoid/reconnection *event catalog* dataset was found by name search in the PDS PPI 'data/' holdings. To assess whether reconnection/plasmoid mass loss balances Enceladus plasma supply using an external event list (from the literature), you can combine Cassini MAG (to identify/characterize reconnection signatures and tail geometry) with CAPS-derived plasma moments and/or RPWS electron densities (to estimate plasma density and flow) and optionally MIMI/CHEMS composition (mean ion mass). Below are the most relevant calibrated/derived PDS4 collections available at PPI and already probed.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary magnetic-field dataset to detect Saturn tail reconnection/plasmoid signatures (e.g., dipolarizations, Bz reversals, current sheet crossings) and to provide context/coordinates for event timing.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Higher-time-resolution MAG needed if your event catalog uses short-duration reconnection signatures; improves plasmoid boundary identification and timing.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides derived ion moments (e.g., density/velocity/temperature) useful for estimating mass density and bulk flow in/near a plasmoid to convert event-by-event volumes into mass loss.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Independent electron density estimates (often robust in tenuous tail plasma) to constrain plasma number density during events; can be paired with composition assumptions to estimate mass density.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI CHEMS Calibrated Full Time Resolution Intensity Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Helps estimate ion composition/mean ion mass (W+ group vs H+) to convert number density to mass density when computing plasmoid mass content.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
  Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
  Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using the mission name as filter keyword.
  Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
  Step 5: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
  Step 6: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
Is the mass loss inferred from the detected Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog sufficient to balance Enceladus as a plasma source?

results.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This data set consists of all of the fits (both good and bad) that were found 
                by Dr. Robert J. Wilson during the NASA Cassini Data Analysis Program grant 
                (NNX12AG90G). The Cassini CAPS plasma data used in the forward model plasma 
                parameter calculations given here came from volume 
                CO-E/J/S/SW-CAPS-2-UNCALIBRATED-V1.0 in the Planetary Data System (PDS).

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini MIMI CHEMS Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini Magnetospheric Imaging Instrument (MIMI) Charge Energy                                             
                Mass Spectrometer (CHEMS) mass per charge, full time resolution intensities and 
                pulse height analysis flux values for 2004-01-01T00:00:00.000Z to 2017-09-15T23:59:59.000Z.

results.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

results.title: CASSINI ORBITER MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED AVERAGE DATA

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: CASSINI ORBITER MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED AVERAGE DATA
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: CASSINI MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED 1 MINUTE AVERAGED ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED Cassini Orbiter Magnetometer Calibrated MAG data in 1 minute averages available covering the period 1999-08-16 (DOY 228) to 2017-09-12 (DOY 255). This volume contains new versions (KRTP, KSO and KSM PRODUCT_VERSION_ID 7 and RTN PRODUCT_VERSION_ID 8) of these data products, processed using an updated calibration. The data are provided in RTN coordinates throughout the mission, with Earth, Jupiter, and Saturn centered coordinates for the respective flybys of those planets. These data have passed PDS peer review. Every effort has been made to ensure that the data and documentation are of the best possible quality. However, users of this data set are encouraged to verify the correctness of the data prior to submitting any publications or other work.

CERTIFIED - This volume contains data and supporting documentation from the Cassini Orbiter CAPS derived ion moments data set, covering 2004-180 to 2012-154. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This collection provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

results.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle

Citation_Information.description:

                magnetic-field data from the Fluxgate Magnetometer and the Vector Helium Magnetomer in Vector mode.
                Data are received in science and housekeeping rates. Additional collections, such as spacecraft attitude, analog, 
                command validation, configuration image, error counter, user-defined engineering data, browse plots, and calibration,
                are also included.

To assess whether reconnection/plasmoid mass loss balances Enceladus plasma supply using an external event list (from the literature), you can combine Cassini MAG (to identify/characterize reconnection signatures and tail geometry) with CAPS-derived plasma moments and/or RPWS electron densities (to estimate plasma density and flow) and optionally MIMI/CHEMS composition (mean ion mass). Below are the most relevant calibrated/derived PDS4 collections available at PPI and already probed.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-1min-ksm","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Min. Avg. Data in KSM Coords. Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary magnetic-field dataset to detect Saturn tail reconnection/plasmoid signatures (e.g., dipolarizations, Bz reversals, current sheet crossings) and to provide context/coordinates for event timing."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mag-cal:data-full-krtp","path":"data/cassini-mag-cal/","title":"Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Higher-time-resolution MAG needed if your event catalog uses short-duration reconnection signatures; improves plasmoid boundary identification and timing."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-caps-derived:data-ion-moments","path":"data/cassini-caps-derived/","title":"Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides derived ion moments (e.g., density/velocity/temperature) useful for estimating mass density and bulk flow in/near a plasmoid to convert event-by-event volumes into mass loss."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-rpws-electron_density:data","path":"data/cassini-rpws-electron_density/","title":"Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Independent electron density estimates (often robust in tenuous tail plasma) to constrain plasma number density during events; can be paired with composition assumptions to estimate mass density."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-mimi-chems-cal:data-cmfull","path":"data/cassini-mimi-chems-cal/","title":"Cassini MIMI CHEMS Calibrated Full Time Resolution Intensity Data Collection","mission":"cassini","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Helps estimate ion composition/mean ion mass (W+ group vs H+) to convert number density to mass density when computing plasmoid mass content."}]}
